title: bradburya triquetra
intro: Bradburya triquetra, commonly known as the Three-angled Bradburya, is a plant species in the family Gesneriaceae. This plant is known for its potential health benefits, which include its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma due to its expectorant properties. The therapeutic actions of Bradburya triquetra include its antimicrobial and antioxidant properties, which can help protect against infections and oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of the plant are still being researched, but they are believed to include compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
side_effects: Bradburya triquetra can have side effects if used improperly, such as dizziness, nausea, and headaches due to its high concentration of toxic compounds. Ingestion of the plant's leaves or flowers can also cause stomach cramps, diarrhea, and vomiting. Prolonged exposure to its extracts can lead to liver and kidney damage, as well as allergic reactions such as skin rashes and itching. In severe cases, improper use of Bradburya triquetra can even cause respiratory failure and cardiac arrest.
